concerns.text: Task T010 implements the random intercept model for data generation but fails to explicitly include the mandatory 'cluster-level treatment assignment' logic required by FR-001 and US-01. The task description only mentions generating Y_ij = mu + u_i + e_ij. Without explicit instruction to assign treatment at the cluster level (CRT design), the implementation risks pseudoreplication (observation-level assignment), rendering the baseline invalid.

Task T027 implements timing instrumentation to verify runtime < 6 hours (FR-006), but lacks a corresponding task to enforce the memory constraint (≤ 7 GB RAM) also mandated by FR-006. The plan explicitly cites the 7 GB limit as a hard constraint; missing a task to monitor or enforce memory usage leaves this requirement unaddressed in the implementation plan.

Task T028 handles edge cases for ICC=0 and unbalanced cluster sizes, but there is no explicit task to validate the 'Minimum 50 clusters' constraint defined in the plan.md ('Cluster Constraint'). The plan states this is required for the validity of cluster-robust variance estimators; without a specific task to enforce or validate this minimum cluster count during simulation setup, the robust methods (US-02) may be applied to invalid data configurations.
